deep_dive.title: Beyond Static Evaluation: Co-Evolutionary Mechanisms for LLM-Driven Strategy Evolution in Adversarial Games
deep_dive.abstract: Recent advances in LLM-driven code evolution have enabled automated discovery by iteratively generating and improving programs. However, applying these methods to adversarial multi-agent games introduces a fundamental challenge: the evaluation landscape shifts as strategies improve, causing fixed evaluators to become unreliable and evolution to stagnate. We propose three mechanisms to address this challenge: evaluator co-evolution, which incorporates discovered champions into the opponent pool; hierarchical deep evaluation, which replaces noisy few-game scores with statistically reliable assessments; and weakness pressure, which dynamically up-weights the most difficult opponents to break through plateaus. We implement these mechanisms within FAMOU, a framework built upon the same foundation-model code-evolution paradigm as OpenEvolve and ShinkaEvolve. On the MCTF 2026 3v3 maritime capture-the-flag task, FAMOU consistently outperforms both baselines under two backbone LLMs, achieving the highest combined score (0.526) and the best generalization to unseen opponents (61.7% win rate), while ablations confirm that each mechanism contributes to performance. Notably, the LLM mutation process generates tactical structures entirely absent from the seed strategies -- including lookahead search and adaptive interception -- demonstrating that code-level evolution can produce nontrivial algorithmic innovations in adversarial settings. The FAMOU-evolved strategy further achieved 1st place in the hardware round-robin and 3rd in simulation at the AAMAS 2026 MCTF Competition, validating its real-world transferability. The optimized implementation and corresponding evaluation codes developed through our evolutionary process are available at: https://github.com/1xiangliu1/FAMOU-CoEvo
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: LLM-driven strategy evolution in adversarial games
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 对抗游戏中的LLM驱动策略进化
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 对抗游戏中的LLM驱动策略进化
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 对抗游戏中的LLM驱动策略进化
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes co-evolutionary mechanisms (evaluator co-evolution, hierarchical deep evaluation, weakness pressure) for LLM-driven strategy evolution in adversarial games, addressing evaluation landscape shift.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对对抗性多智能体游戏中LLM驱动的策略进化面临评估景观动态变化的挑战，提出评估器协同进化、分层深度评估和弱点压力三种机制。这些机制通过动态更新对手池、统计可靠性评估和强调困难对手，打破进化停滞。实验验证了方法在游戏策略发现中的有效性。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该论文针对对抗性多智能体游戏中LLM驱动的策略进化面临评估景观动态变化的挑战，提出评估器协同进化、分层深度评估和弱点压力三种机制。这些机制通过动态更新对手池、统计可靠性评估和强调困难对手，打破进化停滞。实验验证了方法在游戏策略发现中的有效性。

deep_dive.title: VISTA: A Versatile Interactive User Simulation Toolkit for Agent Evaluation
deep_dive.abstract: Evaluation remains a critical bottleneck for interactive agent development. Existing evaluation methods often rely on static benchmarks, which fail to capture the dynamic, multi-step nature of agentic behavior and struggle to expose meaningful failure modes. While user-simulation-based evaluation offers a promising alternative, existing simulation frameworks suffer from two major limitations. First, they provide limited mechanisms for evaluating the quality and comprehensiveness of simulated interactions, making it difficult to assess whether a simulator sufficiently explores an agent's capabilities and failure modes. Second, most frameworks are restricted to either UI-only actions or API-only actions, limiting their ability to model the full range of realistic user behaviors. To address these limitations, we propose VISTA, a Versatile Interactive user Simulation Toolkit for Agent evaluation. Our toolkit includes a suite of six metrics for measuring the realism, capability coverage, and interaction effectiveness of simulated interactions. In addition, we develop a hybrid user simulator that integrates both UI-based interactions and API-based interactions, enabling more realistic and comprehensive evaluation across diverse interactive environments. We evaluate VISTA in e-commerce shopping and education customer service settings and demonstrate that it produces more realistic and comprehensive evaluations than existing methods.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Versatile interactive user simulation toolkit for agent evaluation, directly supports player behavior simulation techniques
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 多功能交互式用户仿真工具包，直接支持玩家行为模拟技术
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 多功能交互式用户仿真工具包，直接支持玩家行为模拟技术
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 多功能交互式用户仿真工具包，直接支持玩家行为模拟技术
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: VISTA provides a user simulation toolkit for evaluating interactive agents, supporting both UI and API actions with built-in simulation quality metrics, addressing limitations of static benchmarks.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文提出了VISTA，一个多功能交互式用户仿真工具包，用于评估交互式智能体。它解决了现有仿真框架的两大局限：缺乏对仿真质量的评估机制，以及只能模拟UI或API单一操作类型。VISTA支持混合操作，并提供量化指标来衡量仿真充分性。该工具可直接用于模拟游戏玩家行为，为虚拟游戏玩家建模提供评估基础。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该论文提出了VISTA，一个多功能交互式用户仿真工具包，用于评估交互式智能体。它解决了现有仿真框架的两大局限：缺乏对仿真质量的评估机制，以及只能模拟UI或API单一操作类型。VISTA支持混合操作，并提供量化指标来衡量仿真充分性。该工具可直接用于模拟游戏玩家行为，为虚拟游戏玩家建模提供评估基础。

quick_skim.title: Role-Agent: Bootstrapping LLM Agents via Dual-Role Evolution
quick_skim.abstract: Although Large Language Model (LLM) agents have demonstrated strong performance on complex tasks, their learning is often limited by inefficient interaction feedback and static training environments, which hinder broader generalization. To address these limitations, this paper introduces Role-Agent, \textcolor{black}{a framework} that harnesses a single LLM to function concurrently as both the agent and the environment, enabling a bootstrapped co-evolution. Role-Agent comprises two synergistic components: World-In-Agent (WIA) and Agent-In-World (AIW). In WIA, the LLM acts as the agent and predicts future states after each action; the alignment between predicted and actual states is then used as a process reward, encouraging environment-aware reasoning. In AIW, the LLM analyzes failure modes from failed trajectories and retrieves tasks with similar failure patterns, thereby reshaping the training data distribution for targeted practice. Experiments on multiple benchmarks show that Role-Agent consistently improves performance, yielding an average gain of over 4\% over strong baselines.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: dual-role evolution for LLM agents training with environment prediction rewards
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 双角色进化框架用于LLM代理训练，利用环境预测奖励
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 双角色进化框架用于LLM代理训练，利用环境预测奖励
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 双角色进化框架用于LLM代理训练，利用环境预测奖励
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Role-Agent bootstraps LLM agent learning by having the agent act as both the agent and environment, using predicted state alignment as process reward to enable environment-aware reasoning without external supervision.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有LLM代理在复杂任务上表现强但受限于低效交互反馈和静态训练环境。本文提出Role-Agent框架，让单一LLM同时扮演代理和环境，通过预测未来状态与实际状态的对齐作为过程奖励，促进环境感知推理。该方法在多个基准上显著提升了代理的学习效率和泛化能力。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有LLM代理在复杂任务上表现强但受限于低效交互反馈和静态训练环境。本文提出Role-Agent框架，让单一LLM同时扮演代理和环境，通过预测未来状态与实际状态的对齐作为过程奖励，促进环境感知推理。该方法在多个基准上显著提升了代理的学习效率和泛化能力。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Search for AI agent training in game environments

quick_skim.title: CLaaS: Continual learning as a service for sample efficient online learning
quick_skim.abstract: Deployed large language model agents must adapt to distribution shift in dynamic environments. Ideally, adaptation can be performed from accumulated agent experiences and retain prior capabilities while transferring to future tasks. However, agent actions and environmental transitions can only be sampled once per scenario, as real-world environments cannot be trivially reset. To this end, we investigate an experiential and online continual learning setting in which agents learn from a stream of scenarios. We propose continual learning as-a-service (CLaaS), a system which enables agents to improve during deployment, abstracted behind a chat API. To increase sample efficiency, CLaaS stores rollouts in an experience replay buffer for gradient reuse during asynchronous training. We evaluate CLaaS on an adversarial task, demonstrating that parametric updates lead to superior forward transfer and less forgetting than in-context learning, with replay being a critical choice for sample efficiency.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: continual learning service for agent adaptation in dynamic environments
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 面向动态环境中代理自适应的持续学习服务
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 面向动态环境中代理自适应的持续学习服务
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 面向动态环境中代理自适应的持续学习服务
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: CLaaS provides a continual learning service with experience replay for online agent adaptation, enabling sample-efficient improvement in dynamic environments, directly applicable to game AI training.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: CLaaS提出持续学习即服务，通过经验回放缓冲区实现代理在部署过程中的在线学习。它解决动态环境中分布偏移问题，仅通过一次采样即可利用过去的经验进行梯度复用。该系统通过聊天API提供持续改进能力，特别适用于游戏代理在多变场景中的自适应优化，显著提高了样本效率。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: CLaaS提出持续学习即服务，通过经验回放缓冲区实现代理在部署过程中的在线学习。它解决动态环境中分布偏移问题，仅通过一次采样即可利用过去的经验进行梯度复用。该系统通过聊天API提供持续改进能力，特别适用于游戏代理在多变场景中的自适应优化，显著提高了样本效率。

quick_skim.title: Variational Proximal Policy Optimization
quick_skim.abstract: Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback via Proximal Policy Optimization often suffers from policy mode collapse, brittle exploration loops, and distribution drift. This paper introduces Variational Proximal Policy Optimization (\(\textsc{VP}_2\textsc{O}\)), a particle-based variational inference framework that maps policy optimization to Stein Variational Gradient Descent within a Mixture-of-Experts architecture. By leveraging functional kernels over localized expert prototypes alongside an expert orthogonalization loss, \(\textsc{VP}_2\textsc{O}\) introduces a geometry-based proximal-control mechanism that can reduce reliance on fixed clipping or KL schedules. Our results on a 33B/4B sparse Mixture-of-Experts model show several improvements across complex reasoning benchmarks, establishing a \(+\mathbf{179}\) ELO gain on Codeforces and a \(\mathbf{32\%}\) reduction in token count on AIME mathematical reasoning tasks.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Variational PPO for RL from human feedback, applicable to training AI agents in game environments
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 变分PPO用于从人类反馈中强化学习，可应用于游戏环境中AI智能体训练
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 变分PPO用于从人类反馈中强化学习，可应用于游戏环境中AI智能体训练
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 变分PPO用于从人类反馈中强化学习，可应用于游戏环境中AI智能体训练
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: VP2O maps policy optimization to Stein variational gradient descent with a Mixture-of-Experts architecture, improving exploration and stability in RLHF.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文提出了变分近端策略优化(VP2O)，一个基于粒子的变分推断框架，将策略优化映射到Stein变分梯度下降，并采用混合专家架构。通过功能核和专家正交化损失，引入基于几何的近端控制机制，减少对固定裁剪或KL调度的依赖。该算法在复杂推理基准上取得改进，可应用于游戏AI的训练优化。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文提出了变分近端策略优化(VP2O)，一个基于粒子的变分推断框架，将策略优化映射到Stein变分梯度下降，并采用混合专家架构。通过功能核和专家正交化损失，引入基于几何的近端控制机制，减少对固定裁剪或KL调度的依赖。该算法在复杂推理基准上取得改进，可应用于游戏AI的训练优化。

quick_skim.title: Leveraging Machine-Learned Advice in Strategic Interactions with No-Regret Learners
quick_skim.abstract: We study how an agent in a two-player repeated game can effectively utilize potentially imperfect advice when interacting with a no-regret learner. We characterize the advice landscape by introducing a pseudo-metric to quantify the usefulness of an advice instance. We demonstrate the pseudo-metric's applicability through two forms of advice: simulators and payoff matrix predictions. We then show how an optimizing player, equipped with correctness guarantees on the advice, could leverage simulators to compute approximate Stackelberg strategies more efficiently, reducing the interaction complexity traditionally required and illustrating the power of good advice. Finally, we extend our analysis to settings where the advice does not have any guarantee of correctness. We find that, in general, a player cannot simultaneously guarantee near Stackelberg performance when the advice is approximately accurate and a no-regret condition when the advice is inaccurate. We do show, however, that it is possible for an advice-aided player to weakly dominate their utility in some (coarse)-correlated equilibria.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: advice in repeated games, simulators for Stackelberg strategies
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 重复博弈中的建议利用，模拟器用于Stackelberg策略
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 重复博弈中的建议利用，模拟器用于Stackelberg策略
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 重复博弈中的建议利用，模拟器用于Stackelberg策略
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Studies how an agent in two-player repeated games can use potentially imperfect advice (simulators, payoff predictions) against no-regret learners to reduce interaction complexity.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文研究在两人重复博弈中，智能体如何利用可能不完美的建议（如模拟器和收益矩阵预测）与无遗憾学习对手交互。引入伪度量量化建议的有用性，并展示如何利用模拟器高效计算近似Stackelberg策略，降低交互复杂度。该工作为游戏中的策略学习提供了有效利用外部建议的理论基础。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文研究在两人重复博弈中，智能体如何利用可能不完美的建议（如模拟器和收益矩阵预测）与无遗憾学习对手交互。引入伪度量量化建议的有用性，并展示如何利用模拟器高效计算近似Stackelberg策略，降低交互复杂度。该工作为游戏中的策略学习提供了有效利用外部建议的理论基础。
